components.name: Dipotassium phosphate (K2HPO4)
components.source: cited:Lenski 1991 (Davis-Mingioli DM base) via Barrick lab LTEE recipe
components.evidence: K2HPO4: 5.34 g (MW 174.18) ... Add deionized water to final volume and autoclave (Barrick lab Davis-Mingioli DM recipe reproducing the Lenski et al. 1991 medium).

components.name: Monopotassium phosphate (KH2PO4)
components.source: cited:Lenski 1991 (Davis-Mingioli DM base) via Barrick lab LTEE recipe
components.evidence: KH2PO4: 2 g (MW 136.07). Final medium contains 75.8 mM potassium and 45.3 mM phosphate.

components.name: Ammonium sulfate ((NH4)2SO4)
components.source: cited:Lenski 1991 (Davis-Mingioli DM base) via Barrick lab LTEE recipe
components.evidence: (NH4)2SO4: 1 g (MW 132.08). Final medium contains 15.2 mM ammonium and 8.41 mM sulfate.

components.name: Trisodium citrate dihydrate (sodium citrate, Na3C6H5O7·2H2O)
components.role: chelator / sodium source (Davis added citrate historically; ~11x the glucose molarity)
components.source: cited:Lenski 1991 (Davis-Mingioli DM base) via Barrick lab LTEE recipe
components.evidence: Na3C6H5O7·(H2O)2: 0.5 g (MW 294.10). Final medium contains 5.1 mM sodium and 1.70 mM citrate. DM25 contains citrate at about 11 times the concentration of glucose.

components.name: Magnesium sulfate heptahydrate (MgSO4·7H2O)
components.concentration: 0.1 g/L (1.0 mL of 10% w/v stock per L)
components.source: cited:Lenski 1991 (Davis-Mingioli DM base) via Barrick lab LTEE recipe
components.evidence: MgSO4·7H2O: 1.0 ml of 10% (w/v) separately autoclaved stock. Final medium contains 0.406 mM magnesium.

components.name: Thiamine hydrochloride (vitamin B1)
components.concentration: 2 mg/L (1.0 mL of 0.2% w/v stock per L)
components.source: cited:Lenski 1991 (Davis-Mingioli DM base) via Barrick lab LTEE recipe
components.evidence: Thiamine: 1.0 ml of 0.2% (w/v) filter-sterilized solution (0.0002% w/v final = 2 mg/L). REL606/607 require thiamine for growth in DM.

components.name: D-Glucose
components.source: main_text (concentration) + cited:Lenski 1991 (DM naming convention) via Barrick lab LTEE recipe
components.evidence: …Davis minimal media (Lenski et al. 1991) supplemented with 100 μg/l glucose (DM100). By LTEE convention DMxxx = xxx mg/L glucose (DM25 = 25 mg/L = 250 µl of 10% glucose per L); the paper's 'μg/l' is a units typo, so DM100 = 100 mg/L = 0.555 mM.

media.base_medium: Davis (Davis-Mingioli) minimal medium as used in the E. coli long-term evolution experiment (LTEE), glucose-limited, with thiamine

sources_chased.what: base recipe cited as 'Davis minimal media (Lenski et al. 1991)', Am Nat 138:1315-1341 (ref bib25)
sources_chased.how: WebFetch barricklab.org Davis-Mingioli DM protocol (reproduces the exact Lenski/LTEE recipe) + WebSearch protocols.io/OpenWetWare cross-check

missing_before: Trisodium citrate dihydrate (sodium citrate) — provides citrate + Na (was in current_uncovered)
Dipotassium/monopotassium phosphate and MgSO4·7H2O were present only as free ions in the model exchange list; now resolved to the actual named salts with concentrations
media.notes: Complete defined recipe recovered from the LTEE Davis-Mingioli base medium (Lenski et al. 1991) plus the paper's DM100 glucose modification. Davis minimal medium is a simple defined medium with NO trace-element or multi-vitamin solution added: the open EX_ca2/cobalt2/cu2/fe2/fe3/mn2/zn2 exchanges in current_components are model defaults / trace reagent contaminants and are NOT deliberately-added recipe ingredients (no textual source supports them), so they are excluded from the recipe above. Cultures were grown aerobically (37 C, orbital shaking at 160 rpm in test tubes), so oxygen corrected from 'facultative' to 'aerobic'. Nitrogen = ammonium (from (NH4)2SO4); buffer/phosphate = K2HPO4/KH2PO4; the only vitamin is thiamine.
